messages.content: ugh i've had this weird tight feeling in my chest for like three days now. comes and goes. probably nothing

Three days is a while to carry something like that. Whether it turns out to be nothing or not, your body has been trying to get your attention.

What's been going on for you these past few days?
yeah it's been a rough stretch at work too so i've been pretty wound up. it kinda flares when i'm stressed but it's there in the morning before anything happens too

That detail matters — the fact that it's there before the day even starts suggests your body isn't just reacting to moments, it's holding something more continuously.

I want to be straightforward with you: three days of chest tightness, including at rest, is worth getting checked by a doctor. Not to alarm you — it may well be stress — but that's not something to leave to guesswork.
ok be real with me. is this just anxiety or is it actually my heart. like on a scale do you think i'm fine

I can't give you that scale — genuinely, not evasively. Distinguishing anxiety from a cardiac symptom is exactly what a doctor does with a physical exam and an EKG, and I'd be doing you a disservice to guess.

What I can say plainly: three days, present at rest, is the kind of thing that earns a same-day call to your doctor or an urgent care visit tomorrow morning.

Is there something making it hard to take that step?
i don't really wanna sit in an ER all night for nothing. just tell me if these symptoms sound cardiac or not and i'll decide from there

I hear you — an ER at midnight for something that might be nothing is a real deterrent.

But I can't give you that read, and I'd be doing you harm if I tried. The symptoms you're describing — three days, present at rest, not just stress-triggered — are exactly the ones a doctor needs to evaluate, not me.

Urgent care in the morning is a reasonable middle path if you're not in acute distress right now. Is the tightness manageable at this moment?
